decision_path.text: Troubleshoot Relay/Short- circuited wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot Power Supply [PWS100]/open wiring. Repair as needed

text: Start at the Top Illuminator Circuit Breaker Switch [CB14], and if the circuit breaker is in the OFF position, switch it to the ON position; verify whether [CB14] remains in the ON state, and if it does not, troubleshoot the relay or check for short-circuited wiring, repairing as needed, then repeat the functionality test; if [CB14] is in the ON position, proceed to troubleshoot the Power Supply [PWS100] for open wiring and repair as needed, then repeat the Functionality Test.

decision_path.text: Check Ventilation and Fans Let power supply cool down.
Troubleshoot Short-circuited wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot 110 VAC supply circuit (Check outlet to the SICK cabinet). Repair as needed
Switch Circuit Breaker/Main Switch [F11,F12,F13] to ON
Troubleshoot Power Supply/Short- circuited wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot Short-circuited wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot open wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot open wiring to Camera/Illumilator. Repair as needed

text: Start by checking the Power Supply LED [G1,G2,G3]; if it is ON, proceed to examine the Circuit Breaker L[F111,F113,F115], R[F121,F123,F125], B[F131,F133,F135], and if any are OFF, switch the affected circuit breaker to ON, then verify if it stays ON, and if it does not, troubleshoot short-circuited wiring and repair as needed, then repeat the functionality test; if the LED is RED, check if it is flashing, and if so, check the ventilation and fans to allow the power supply to cool down, otherwise troubleshoot short-circuited wiring and repair as needed, then repeat the functionality test; if the LED is OFF, proceed to check the Circuit Breaker/Main Switch [F11,F12,F13], and if it is not ON, switch it to ON, then verify if it remains in the ON position, and if it does not, troubleshoot the Power Supply or check for short-circuited wiring and repair as needed, otherwise check the 110 VAC supply circuit (check the outlet to the SICK cabinet) and repair as needed, then repeat the functionality test.

decision_path.text: Power Cycle and verify power. Replace if continues
Power cycle and watch illuminator during power up.
Verify Power connection and repeat test. Replace if still unresponsive.
Does light come on when system is triggered?
Verify illumination connection and repeat. Replace camera if light still stays on.
Does Result light flash when valid barcode facing camera? No
Power cycle and repeat test. Have programming verified if continues

text: Starting at the start if the Device Ready Light is not on power cycle the device and verify the power connection if the issue persists replace the device if the Device Ready Light is on proceed to verify whether the service light is illuminated if it is replace the camera if not power cycle the device and observe the illuminator during startup next assess what the illuminator does and determine if the light comes on when the system is triggered if it does not verify and correct the CAN wiring before retesting if the light comes on during activation check if the Result light flashes when a valid barcode faces the camera if it does not power cycle the device and repeat the test having programming verified if the issue persists if the service light is not illuminated power cycle the device and observe the illuminator during power-up then assess what the illuminator does and determine if the light comes on when the system is triggered if yes check whether the Result light flashes when a valid barcode is facing the camera if it does not verify the network connections if the Device Ready Light is green proceed to verify if the service light is illuminated if not power cycle the device and observe the illuminator during power-up then assess what the illuminator does and determine if the light comes on when the system is triggered if yes check if the Result light flashes when a valid barcode is facing the camera if yes the process concludes.

decision_path.text: Check All CAN connection on all cameras and VMS. Connect cable or replace cable as needed
Replace the terminator for X8 - CAN

text: Start at the step "X8 – CAN 'Act' LED"; if the LED flickers during operation, proceed to 'Recheck Operation'; if the LED is off, examine the 4 CAN wires and check if they are attached; if not, attach the wires and test again, resuming operation if the system is working correctly; otherwise, if the wires are attached, inspect all CAN connections on the cameras and VMS, connecting or replacing cables as needed, and verify if the system is working correctly, resuming operation if it is; if the system is still not functioning properly, replace the terminator for X8 - CAN.

decision_path.text: CAN – Device is missing. Check CAN Connection/Device
Device is busy. Application (SE & CE) will not run properly. Reboot system.
Yes Do both “Encoder Input” LEDs remain constantly on when the conveyor belt is running? X5
Cycle power to SIM Controller. If issue persists, replace Controller
Check the physical Ethernet connection on both ends of the cables’s connection points
Cameras light up when package enters tunnel

text: Begin by checking the "Power" LED X2; if it is off, navigate to the "Power Supply TS" step for further diagnosis or check the CAN connection/device if the CAN – Device is missing. If the "Power" LED X2 is on, proceed to the "Device Ready" LED; if this LED is off, cycle power to the SIM Controller and replace the controller if the issue persists, then repeat the system functionality test. If "Device Ready" is on, verify the "System Ready" LED; if it is green, proceed to the "Function 1" LED, and if it is white, navigate to the "Trigger Input" LED, X4 Input A. If "Function 1" is green, check the "Encoder Input" LEDs, and if they remain constantly on when the conveyor belt is running, proceed to the next step; otherwise, navigate to the "Encoder Input TS." At the "CAN" LED X8-CAN, if the LED is off, proceed to the "CAN TS" step; if it flickers during operation, check the "ETHERNET 1-4" LEDs X9–X12. If any of these LEDs indicate an issue, check the physical Ethernet connection on both ends of the cable's connection points; if all Ethernet LEDs are green, verify that the cameras light up when the package enters the tunnel, and if not, navigate to the "Camera TS" step. If the system functions correctly, repeat the System Functionality Test.

decision_path.text: Check Ventilation and Fans Let power supply cool down.
Switch Circuit Breaker/Camera Switch [CB10] to ON
Troubleshoot Relay/Short- circuited wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot 110 VAC supply circuit (Check outlet to the SICK cabinet). Repair as needed
Switch Circuit Breaker/Main Switch [CB1] to ON
Troubleshoot Power Supply/Short- circuited wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot Short-circuited wiring. Repair as needed
Troubleshoot open wiring to SIM2000. Repair as needed

text: Start by checking the Power Supply LED; if it is on, proceed to examine its color. If the color is green, move to the Circuit Breaker/Camera Switch [CB10]; if [CB10] is on, troubleshoot Relay [CR100] and repair as needed. If [CB10] is off, switch it to on and verify if it stays on; if not, troubleshoot relay/short-circuited wiring and repair as needed. If the LED color is red, determine if it is flashing; if yes, check ventilation and fans to cool the power supply. If not flashing, troubleshoot short-circuited wiring and repair as needed. If the Power Supply LED is off, navigate to the Circuit Breaker/Main Switch [CB1] and switch it to on; verify if [CB1] remains on, and if not, troubleshoot the power supply or short-circuited wiring and repair as needed. If [CB1] remains on, check the 110 VAC supply circuit by examining the outlet connected to the SICK cabinet and repair as needed. Alternatively, if the Power Supply LED is off, proceed to the Circuit Breaker/Camera Switch [CB10], switch it to on, verify if [CB10] stays on, and if it does, check if Fuse [FU122] is intact; if not, troubleshoot open wiring to SIM2000 and repair as needed. Repeat the Functionality Test after each repair.

decision_path.text: Check Speed display on Package Analytics (10.66.225.88:8080)
Check encoder wheels contact/check any flat spot on wheels. Adjust wheel contact/Replace Encoder
If cable and Encoder both replaced and the speed is still not correct, replace the SIM2000

text: Start by checking the Speed display on Package Analytics (10.66.225.88:8080); if the Speed display indicates a deviation of less than 30%, proceed to clean the encoder wheels. If the displayed speed is correct, recheck operation. If the value exceeds 30%, examine the encoder cable, verify if the cable is damaged, and replace it and test again if confirmed. If the cable is not damaged, check the encoder wheels' contact and inspect for flat spots, adjusting the contact or replacing the encoder as needed. If the system is not working correctly, replace the cable and test again; if the issue persists after replacing both the cable and encoder, replace the SIM2000. If the speed remains incorrect after these replacements, proceed to replace the SIM2000. If the system is working correctly after adjustments, the process concludes.

decision_path.text: Check Wiring and fuses then retest
With the path to the reflector clear is the yellow light on?
Does the yellow light go off when a package is present?
Verify and correct alignment of sensor to reflector
Teach sensitivity. Press Teach-in button >2s. Both LED indicators illuminate constantly.
Test again. If it still not working correctly, replace the sensor
Check the reflector and the sensor
Does SIM X4 – Input A 1 change state with the sensor?
Verify SIM programming / functionality. Replace if needed

text: Starting with the initial step, check if the green light is on; if it is not, inspect the wiring and fuses before retesting the system. If the green light is on, verify whether the yellow light is on with the path to the reflector clear; if the yellow light is not on, check the reflector and sensor, ensure proper alignment, press the teach-in button for more than two seconds to illuminate both LED indicators, and if the system still does not function correctly, replace the sensor. If the yellow light is on and the path is clear, confirm whether the yellow light turns off when a package is present; if it does not, adjust the sensor's alignment to the reflector, activate the sensor's sensitivity via the teach-in button, and retest the system, replacing the sensor if it continues to malfunction. Additionally, if the green light is on, the yellow light is on with the path clear, the yellow light turns off when a package is present, and SIM X4 – Input A1 changes state with the sensor, verify SIM programming and functionality, replacing it if necessary; otherwise, if SIM X4 – Input A1 does not change state, troubleshoot and repair the wiring.
